title: Ops Risk Triage Verdict
description: Layer 2 LLM classifier output for a single Bash command. Slice 1 of v2.2 ships this schema but does NOT yet invoke the classifier; the hook's observe-mode log uses a different (looser) shape. Slice 2 will wire runner/ops-triage-classify.sh to emit objects conforming to this schema. Strict-mode compliant: additionalProperties:false requires every property in required (v2.1.0 Bug 1 lesson).

risk.description: Single risk category. 'unknown' MUST be treated as escalate, never as safe — the 'unknown is not safe' rule (see PROPOSAL-ops-risk-triage.md §1).

confidence.description: 5 = trivially certain. A 'safe_readonly' or 'local_read' verdict below 4 MUST be treated as escalate by the hook (bias to escalate on low confidence).

escalate_to_codex.description: True for anything that mutates state, reads externally, or is unknown — i.e. anything Layer 3 (Codex ops-preflight deep review) should look at before the operator approves.

reason.description: One short factual sentence explaining the classification. No speculation, no fix suggestions, no conversational prose. Goes into the operator's ask prompt and the observe log.
